texts.full_text: Many questions have since been asked about how much Epstein’s famous friends knew of his activitiesand whether they were complicit in any way. Some have suggested that Epstein's network of high-profile friends and contacts, including former presidents, politicians, and celebrities, may have provided him with some form of protection or assistance. However, there is no concrete evidence to support these claims. The arrest of Epstein has also sparked debates about the extent of his influence and the potential involvement of others in his crimes. Critics argue that the justice system's previous leniency towards Epstein, including a non-prosecution agreement in 2008 that allowed him to plead guilty only to a lesser charge of sex trafficking, highlights systemic issues within the legal system that may have enabled powerful individuals to avoid accountability. In the wake of Epstein's arrest, there has been renewed interest in investigating the roles of his high-profile associates.

texts.full_text: jii:2010is; @Homma:2011pg; @Homma:2012mn], named “Quasi parallel scattering” (QPS), is the collision of two copropagating lasers. The natural angular divergence of the lassered by the authors, in which the stimulated photon-photon scattering process leads to a signal that could be detected with current technology. However, upon closer examination of the luminosity calculations presented in these works, we have identified a flaw that could significantly affect the feasibility of detecting dark matter through this method. The luminosity, which is a measure of the number of interactions per unit time and area, is crucial for estimating the signal strength and thus the detectability of the dark matter signal. The flaw in the luminosity calculations arises from an incorrect treatment of the photon bunching effect. In the proposed scheme, two identical laser pulses are designed to collide head-on, with the goal of enhancing the probability of photon-photon interactions. The key idea is that by matching the temporal and spatial profiles of the two laser pulses, the effective number of photons available for interaction at any given moment can be increased, thus boosting the signal. However, the existing calculations overlook the fact that the photons in each pulse are not perfectly synchronized or aligned. This leads to a lower effective luminosity than what is calculated, as the overlap between the two pulses in both time and space is not maximized. The discrepancy becomes even more pronounced when considering the high-energy regime, where the number of interactions is highly sensitive to the overlap quality. To illustrate this point, let's consider a simplified model where the photons are distributed uniformly within each laser pulse. texts.full_text: You: Place Card Ideas My mom rolled her eyes a few times at me while planning our wedding. Granted, I was pretty annoying. I even schlepped her all the way across the country wanting to see venues in Napa. But once we decided on Rhode Island, I had a vision. I wanted our rustic chic wedding to be personal and warm. Often, my mom would say “Don’t you think you’re going a little overboard?” So when she called to ask what I was thinking of as place cards for our rehearsal dinner, I took a deep breath and gave her the answer I thought she wanted. “We can make it easy and ask the invitation designer to make matching cards?” It killed me to utter those words. Mom said, “Honey, we can do better than that. Maybe collect rocks or shells from the beach and write everyone’s names on them?” If only Mom could have seen the shit-eating grin on my face at that moment. She had joined Team DIY. The rock is hidden here, but the flowers turned out beautiful too. texts.full_text: ects aged 18-80 years. The respondents living in areas with L den < 60 dB(A) were designated as the reference group. Odds for self-reported CHD in relation to traffic noise exposure were estimated by univariate and multifactorial logistic regression with adjustments for potential confounders and effect modifiers. The adjusted odds ratio (OR) for self-reported CHD was 1.72 (95 % CI 1.36-2.19) per 5 dB(A) increase of L den (range 55-80 dB(A)). A gender-related risk difference was observed among male (OR 1.47 (1.07-2.02)) and female (OR 1.83 (1.27-2.65)) respondents. A stronger effect for subjects in the age group 55-64 years old was found, with age, residence period, body mass index, and self-reported stress being significant confounders. This study suggests epidemiological evidence that exposure to road traffic noise of L den > 65 dB(A) may be associated with occurrence of CHD in adult subjects. texts.full_text: g. During reentry on February 1, hot gasses entered the wing through the damaged area of the orbiter’s thermal protection system, causing devastating structural failure that led to the destruction of Columbia and the deaths of the seven crew members onboard. After the Columbia disaster, NASA grounded the space shuttles for more than a year as it worked on new safety protocols to ensure that such a tragedy would not happen again. As part of the preparations for the Return to Flight mission, the Agency required a method for detecting potentially hazardous defects in the external tank’s sprayed-on insulating foam prior to launch. Partnership NASA Langley Research Center scientists suspected that a new imaging technology called terahertz imaging had the potential to accurately find flaws in the foam on the external tank. Terahertz radiation—lying between microwaves and far infrared on the el...electric spectrum, is capable of penetrating solid materials with minimal absorption or scattering. The NASA Langley Research Center scientists partnered with researchers from other institutions to develop a terahertz imaging system that could be used for this purpose. The team designed an instrument called Terahertz Imaging System (TIS) and conducted experiments using it on the external tank's insulating foam at various temperatures, including those similar to launch conditions. The results of these tests showed promising potential for TIS in detecting defects within the insulation material before a space shuttle launched into orbit. The system was able to detect small cracks or voids that could potentially compromise structural integrity during flight by emitting terahertz radiation through its lens and analyzing reflections from various angles, providing detailed images with high resolution. texts.full_text: Cr levels significantly decreased after antimuscarinic treatment [12], and were significantly reduced in responders in studies [13,15]. However, we observed different results. The study demonstrated that urinary NGF/Cr levels tended to decrease in responders, including both recurrent and nonrecurrent patients, but this change was not statistically significant. Patients who did not experience recurrence showed a significant decrease in urinary NGF/Cr levels after treatment. Urinary NGF/Cr appeared to decrease only in those who did not experience recurrence. This finding is the main strength of our study, suggesting that medication cessation should be considered only in those who maintain lowered urinary NGF/Cr levels compared to baseline by the end of treatment. Interestingly, in the recurrence group, the urinary NGF/Cr level showed a tendency to increase after treatment, despite the response to antimuscarinics. This result implies that an unknown mechanism of anticholinergics may have been present in the no recurrence group. The mechanism through which the urinary level of NGF increases in OAB patients is not yet established. Stretching of the bladder smooth muscle cells increased the expression of messenger RNA for NGF and stimulated the secretion of NGF in vitro [17]. A decreased urinary level of NGF after antimuscarinic treatment might be related to the suppression of bladder smooth muscle as a result of OAB symptom improvement [15]. Further studies to confirm these results, as well as research into the precise mechanism, are required. The finding that a significant decrease in urinary NGF/Cr is predictive that no recurrence will take place after treatment could help physicians to make the decision to stop antimuscarinic treatment. If you had intercourse around that time frame—especially within those critical few days before and after ovulation—you definitely have a good chance at conception. It's understandable to feel excited once things get moving in this direction! Your fiancé might be more cautious or less optimistic, but it’s great for both of you to stay positive. "This ruling undermines the foundational principle that property rights are sacred and should not be trampled by government for the benefit of private interests," Dix stated during a press conference in Des Moines. His proposed legislation, House File 217, would amend Iowa’s eminent domain laws to explicitly prohibit the use of condemnation for private development projects. The bill would also require local governments to demonstrate "blight" in a specific area before allowing any seizure of property, a standard critics argue would be difficult to meet. Additionally, the measure would mandate public hearings and community input before any condemnation proceedings could begin. Dix, who has long advocated for property rights, argued that the Supreme Court’s decision in created a dangerous precedent. "When the courts side with government over homeowners, it sends a message that citizens are powerless," he said. "This bill is about restoring balance and ensuring that Iowans can trust their rights are protected." The proposal has drawn mixed reactions. Urban development groups and some business leaders expressed concern that the restrictions could stifle economic growth, particularly in struggling communities. It includes properties and methods necessary for managing the connection to a database, executing queries, and disconnecting from it. ### Key Components: 1. Properties: - `$link`: A resource representing the active database link. - `$connection_params`: An array containing parameters used for establishing the connection (e.g., host, username, password). - `$database_name`: The name of the connected database. 2. Constructor (`__construct`): Initializes a new instance and establishes a connection using provided parameters by calling `connect`. 3. Abstract Methods: - `connect($params)`: Establishes a connection to the database with given parameters. - `query($query)`: Executes an SQL query on the connected database. - `disconnect()`: Closes the active database link. 4. Public Methods: - `getConnectionParams()`: Returns the current connection parameters. - `getDatabaseName()`: Retrieves the name of the currently connected database. Huntington's disease (HD) is a neurodegenerative disorder characterized by a progressive motor and cognitive decline and the development of psychiatric symptoms. The origin of molecular and biochemical disturbances in HD is a mutation in the HTT gene, which is autosomally dominantly inherited. The altered huntingtin protein is ubiquitousand can affect multiple cellular processes including energy metabolism. Energy production within cells occurs primarily through aerobic respiration where glucose molecules are converted into ATP (adenosine triphosphate), the primary source of chemical power for cell functions, by mitochondria. In Huntington's disease patients' brains and peripheral tissues have been observed to exhibit dysregulation in energy metabolism. texts.full_text: mental deterioration that characterizes it, is undesirable. Potential economic and social difficulties notwithstanding, living longer and healthier lives is a positive and productive premise. Acceptance of the preceding premise leads to the conclusion that the technology that would facilitate radical life extension ought to be pursued. Dr. Aubrey De grey, a Cambridge gerontologist with a formidable beard, offers the argument that it would be morally dubious of us not to develop these technologies and deprive future generations of the benefits therein. To have the ability to develop therapies that lead to longer and healthier lives makes it our responsibility to do so. Policy decisions concerning the adoption and implementation of the policies should be left to those generations in which they are most relevant; it is presumptuous of us to make those decisions for them by not developing these therapies. In his view, it is not only desirable but a moral imperative to give future generations the choice and allow them to decide on implementation. Our hesitation in diverting resources to the development of these theraPies is actually condemning future generations to a life span that is far shorter than it could be were we to actively pursue life extension technology. Furthermore, we cannot presume to fully understand the social and political landscape of the future and their priorities – they will be best suited to make a choice and we should allow them to do so. Without going so far as to characterize those opposed to life extension technology as luddites, there is a serious risk of preventing the potential benefits of these technologies from reaching us, or our children, in time. Some people’s fear of what may happen if people live ever longer lives is not a basis for pre-emptive policy decisions. texts.full_text: PY - 2017/3/18/entrez SP - 1 EP - 10 JF - Journal of biophysics and structural acoustics JO - J Biophys Struct Interact VL - 15 IS - 2 N2 - Alarin, a regulatory peptide, belongs to the galanin family and plays the same regulatory roles as galanin in orexigenic activity and energy metabolism. Our previous studies had found that galanin might facilitate insulin sensitivity via activation of its central receptors. To date, little is known about whether central alarin may exert similar effects on insulin sensitivity. In order to investigate this, alarin and its specific antagonist, alarin 6-25Cys, were administered into the cerebral ventricles of type 2 diabetic rats (T2DR) to evaluate the changes in insulin resistance. The results indicated that central treatment with alarin significantly increased the body weight of animals, the 2-(N-(7-nitrobenz-2-oxa-1,3-diazol-4-yl)amino)-2-deoxyglucose uptake, the plasma adiponectin levels, the glucose infusion rates in hyperinsulinemic-euglycemic clamp tests, the vesicle-associated membrane protein 2 as well as glucose transporter 4 (GLuT4 (SLC2A4)) protein and mRNA levels, and the ratios of GLU-4 expression to β-actin. Alarin antagonist reversed these effects caused by central alarin, suggesting that exogenous administration of alarin in T2DR increased insulin resistance via activation of its receptors. texts.full_text: in Finland.\[[1](#CIT0001)\] The aims of the reform will be to even out differences in health and wellbeing and to curb cost increases. Health and social services will be organised in a more customer-oriented way, and primary health and social services as well as specialised hospital care will be administered and funded by the same regional authority. The key objectives of the reform are to promote the health, wellbeing and social safety of the population, ensure equal access to services, and enhance primary care. Diabetes type 2 is one of the most common chronic illnesses in Finland as well as in other developed countries. The importance of diabetes to public health is mainly based on the complications associated with the disease. People with diabetes run a significantly higher risk of cardiovascular diseases.\[[2](#CIT0002)\] The city of Oulu is located in northern Finland and has about 200,000 inhabitants. It has been calculated that 10% of the citizens account for 81% of the social and health care expenditure.\[[3](CIT0003)\] The city of Oulu is currently in the process of planning and implementing the health and social services reform, and the management of diabetes care is one of the key areas of focus. The reform aims to integrate diabetes care services and improve the management of the condition to reduce the burden of diabetes and its complications, improve the health and wellbeing of the population, and reduce health care expenditure. texts.full_text: H. Becquerel discovered penetrating radiation. In 1897, J.J. Thomson showed that electrons have negative electric charge and come from ordinary matter. For matter to be electrically neutral, there must also be positive charges lurking somewhere. Where are and what carries these positive charges? A monumental breakthrough came in 1911 when Ernest Rutherford and his coworkers conducted an experiment that involved bombarding a thin gold foil with alpha particles—positively charged helium nuclei. Most of the alpha particles passed through the foil without much deflection, but some were scattered at large angles or even bounced back toward their source. This surprising result suggested to Rutherford and his team that atoms are not solid spheres as previously thought; instead, they must have a structure where most of the mass is concentrated in a tiny central core. This dense region came to be known as the nucleus, which contains all the positive charge (carried by protons) and almost all of the atom's mass. The electrons were proposed to orbit around this positively charged nucleus at various distances—much like planets revolving around the sun—but with significant differences due to quantum mechanical effects that would later become apparent. This model became known as Rutherford’s nuclear model or simply, the planetary model of the atom. However, there was a major issue: according to classical physics, such an arrangement should be unstable because orbiting electrons would continuously radiate energy and spiral into the nucleus within fractions of a second. It took Niels Bohr's introduction of quantum postulates in 1913 to provide stability by proposing that only certain orbits are allowed where electron angular momentum is quantized. This early atomic model laid the groundwork for further developments, including understanding isotopes (atoms with different numbers of neutrons), and eventually led to a more complete picture involving subatomic particles like neutrons discovered in 1932. The exploration into nuclear physics had just begun, setting the stage for revolutionary changes not only in our fundamental view of matter but also leading to practical applications such as medical imaging techniques (e.g., PET scans) and energy production through nuclear reactions. texts.full_text: there any way to see a log of it? Here is my code ALTER TRIGGER [dbo].[SUBSID_INSERT] on [dbo].[STUDENT_MASTER] AFTER INSERT AS BEGIN SET NOCOUNT ON DECLARE @numrows int, @hostname char(30), @sysuser char(30); SELECT @numrows = @@rowcount; IF @numrows = 0 RETURN; SELECT @hostname = substring(hostname, 1, 30) FROM master.dbo.sysprocesses WHERE spid = @@spid; SELECT @sysuser = substring(system_user, 1, 30); INSERT INTO insert statement, FROM STUDENT_MASTER JOIN INSERTED I ON STUDENT_MASTER.ID_NUM = I.ID_NUM JOIN NAME_MASTER ON NAME_MASTER.ID_NUM = STUDENT_MASTER.ID_NUM WHERE STUDENT_MASTER.id_num not in ( select DISTINCT id_num from StudentProgramMaster) and StudentProgramStatus='Active'; END;You can try checking the syntax of your trigger by using SSMS(SQL Server Management Studio): Steps are as follows: Right-click on database -> Tasks-> Script Object. Choose "Trigger" from dropdown menu, then select [SUBSID_INSERT] in object explorer pane and click OK to view its script. Check for any syntax errors or missing commands like 'END' etc After fixing those issues if present run the updated trigger again which should solve your problem of after insert not working issue. Also ensure that there are no blocking/permission related factors preventing it from executing properly such as appropriate roles,schemas assigned to user who tries execute this command on database

texts.full_text: ate of the Art: Studies in angiogenesis link new blood vessel formation to both wound healing and pathologies in the human body. Angiogenesis can be stimulated during regeneration of diseased or damaged tissues as well as during tumor growth and metastasis, in order to gain access to oxygen and nutrients necessary for survival. Gene therapy is a promising field to combat human disease by integrating new healthy gene products into a diseased or damaged state to influence recovery or impede disease progression. The Global gene therapy market is still in the experimental stages with only a few products in Phase II/III clinical trials. One gene therapy drug, Gendicine, has been approved and is marketed in China. Overall the industry is predicted to reach $480 million by 2015. Disadvantages with the Current Art: Companies moving to develop gene therapy technologies would benefit from a built-in modifier or a controlled, targeted aspect of the delivery of the therapeutic gene product. Otherwise irrevocable genetic changes made to the patient’s cells can have detrimental effects. Advantages of Invention: The inventors have engineered an optimized version (referred herein as OPTI) containing a shortened core enhancer element, with reduced likelihood of silencing over time in target human arterial smooth muscle cell cultures compared to previously known constructs which rely on viral sequences for efficacy. When coupled through the use or identification and employment if specific promoters these elements have strong but restricted activity within targeted artery tissues (human aorta). This provides novel avenues allowing safer delivery where traditionally difficult; targeting specifically developing arteries while also protecting other more sensitive organs like brain, heart

The increase in the proportion who have never been married is particularly striking: from one-in-twelve adults over age twenty-five back then — a mere eight per cent— compared with nearly two out-of-eleven now 19%. The number of women voters has also increased, and they represent an important voting bloc. In fact in the election cycle (2008) single female turnout was than that for married females. The marriage gap is a real phenomenon — but it’s not just about marital status; there are other factors at play as well such us age , race/ethnicity and education level . For example : younger voters tend to be unmarried ; African Americans have rates of both divorce AND marriages compared with whites . So what does this mean for politicians? Well first off – if you want our vote then start paying attention! We’re not going anywhere anytime soon so get used it! Spring 2019 at The Apex The Apex’s extensive programme of famous faces and less well-known known musicians means there’ll something going on to suit most tastes next year… Spring’s eclectic line-up includes Paul Young’s band Los Pacaminos, who will play the very best in Tex Mex Border music on 11 January. From The Jam sees Bruce Foxton & co celebrating the 40th Anniversary of ‘All Mod Cons’ on 17 January. Accomplished star of the West End and Broadway Stage, Ramin Karimloo returns with his acclaimed Broadgrass Band on 29 January. One of the most popular and exciting live rhythm and blues acts, Dr Feelgood return on 13 February. Dub-dance pioneers Dreadzone return with their unique blend of electro, dub and reggae on 1 March; Steve Harley & his Acoustic Trio are back on 14 March; John Illsley brings Dire Straits’ music and memories on 25 March; and blues-rock guitar prodigy Chantel McGregor returns on 29 March. Although there is a wide variety of genres on offer, fiddler Alasdair Fraser’s Spring album ‘Footloose & Fancy Free’, which also features Scottish songstress Katie McNally plays The Apex for the first time, in April (6th). Also making an anticipated return are top UK acoustic trio Josie Lawson with Lucy Ward and Lisa Knapp on 12 February. There is no better venue than the Bury St Edmunds Apex as it combines excellent sight-lines and sound. texts.full_text: need updates as well. #### `ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush` When a component uses the strategy (`@Component({ changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush })`), it changes how that particular component is checked for updates. Here’s what happens: 1. Input Reference Check: - The framework only checks components marked with `ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPass` when an input reference has changed. - This means if the object or primitive passed to a child via @Input() does not change (i.e., its memory address remains the same), Angular will skip this component during check cycles. 2. Events and Change Detection: - Even with `OnPush`, certain events can still trigger checks in components. - Events that originate from within an decorator or a child of such a component (e.g., user interactions like clicks, form submissions). - Direct changes to the input properties via direct assignment. 3. Immutable Data: - For `OnPush` strategy to work efficiently, you should use immutable data structures. - If an object is mutable and its internal state can change without changing references (e.g., modifying a property of an array or object), Angular won't detect these changes unless the entire referenc

texts.full_text: secretary Matt Hancock set the target on April 2. Figures he gave at the daily briefing today stated that testing figures had hit 122,347 on April 30. However, some questions remain about whether the target has actually been hit. RespondinResponding to these claims involves looking closely into how those tests are being counted and what criteria define a "test." Here's an explanation of potential considerations: 1) Test Count Criteria: The government’s testing figures could include different types of COVID-19 test results, such as Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) swab tests or lateral flow rapid antigen tests. It might also count both confirmed positive and negative cases in the daily total. 2) Testing Locations and Accessibility: - How accessible are these 100,000+ testing locations to different parts of society? Are they evenly distributed across urban and rural areas? 3) Turnaround Times: Another critical factor is how quickly test results come back. Lengthy delays in receiving a result can undermine the effectiveness of widespread screening. 4) Capacity for Testing: - The number of people who are willing to get tested, whether they exhibit symptoms or not. - Whether there's enough capacity among healthcare professionals and laboratories to process all these tests without bottlenecks forming which could slow down both testing throughput rates as well as result turnaround times. 5) Test Accuracy: Not every test is created equal in terms of accuracy. Some may have higher false negative/positive ratios than other

texts.full_text: scribed species in three genera distributed in China and Southeast Asia [@pone.0109140-Liang1]. Commonly known as froghoppers or spittlebugs, species of the Cercopoidea are characterized by their extremely powerful jumping ability [@pone.0109140-Burrows1], [@pone.0109140-Gorb1] and the habit of covering themselves with foamy saliva-like masses during their nymphal stages. The foams are secreted by a specialized bellow-like tube structure on the ventral surface of the abdomen of the nymphs and the foam itself is formed by mucopolysaccharides and proteins secreted by the glandular segment of the Malpighian tubules mixing with air [@pone.0109140-Rakitov1]. Spittlebugs are strict plant feeders and many species exhibit strong preferences for nitrogen-fixing plants [@pone.0109140-thompson1], [@pone.0109140-Thompson2], while some species are grass pests which can cause severe economic damage [@pone.0109140-Li1]. Currently, the taxonomy and phylogenetic relationships within Callitettixini remain poorly understood due to limited morphological data and lack of molecular studies. This thesis aims to provide a comprehensive understanding of the systematics and evolutionary history of this tribe by integrating both morphological and molecular approaches. The primary objectives are: (1) To examine the external and internal morphology of all described species within Callitettixini, focusing on characters that have been previously shown to be useful in cercopid systematics; (2) To generate DNA sequence data for multiple loci from representatives of each genus, including both mitochondrial and nuclear genes; (3) To reconstruct the phylogenetic relationships within Callitettixini using maximum likelihood and Bayesian methods based on combined morphological and molecular dataset

On Sunday night, thousands of blue- and green-clad Seahawks fans took to the streets of Seattle to celebrate, with revelers downtown lighting bonfires while fans on Greek Row near the University of Washington set fire to couches in what police said were mostly non-violent, if boisterous, celebrations. The atmosphere was dampened somewhat by two non-life-threatening shootings and by revelers who threw bottles at police and storefront windows downtown as the party wound down early on Monday, said Seattle police spokesman Mark Jamieson. Police made about a half-dozen arrests, he said. “The overall atmosphere for the vast majority of the night was one of just celebration,” Jamieson said. “People were ecstatic that their team won.” The Seahawks were enthusiastically embraced throughout the Pacific Northwest. texts.full_text: of a terminal malignant process. We describe a endovascular desobliteration of a subtotal occluded SVC and the left innominate vein using a technique in a 68-year-old male patient with metastatic lung cancer. The procedure was carried out under local anesthesia with ultrasound guidance. A 10 Fr sheath was introduced into the right internal jugular vein and a 6 Fr guiding catheter was advanced over a 0.035-inch wire into the left innominate vein. Through this, a 9 mm × 40 mm balloon (Sequess, Cook Medical) was used to predilate the stenotic segment of the innominate vein. A 14 mm × 40 mm balloon (Sequess, Cook Medical) was then used to predilate the vena cava stenosis. A 10 mm × 40 mm stentgraft (Viabahn, Abbott Vascular) was deployed in the superior vena cava from the junction of the left innominate vein to the right atrium. The technique was then performed by deploying a 14 mm × 40 mm stentgraft (Viabahn, Abbott Vascular) in the left innominate vein from the superior vena cava stentgraft junction to the left brachiocephalic vein. The post-procedure venogram showed no residual stenosis and normal flow in both veins. The patient was asymptomatic post-procedure with resolution of his upper limb edema. The procedure described here provides a safe, effective, and less alternative to open surgical bypass for the management of

texts.full_text: A syringe is used in dentistry for controlled delivery of liquid, air, or a combination of liquid and air, to the mouth of the patient. A syringe generally includes a syringe head that is manipulated by the operator and connected by flexible conduits to a source of water and compressed air. A tubular, elongated syringe tip is connected to the syringe head. A conventional syringe tip comprises an inner tube and a concentric outer tube. The inner tube mates at one end with a liquid-outlet port in the syringe head, thereby providing a conduit for the liquid from the syringe head to the free end of the syringe tip.
